title: Extended Warehouse Management (EWM)
learning_objectives: Understand the purpose of SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM).
Learn how warehouse operations are managed in SAP.
Understand warehouse structure and storage concepts.
Identify common SAP EWM transaction codes.
Learn how EWM integrates with manufacturing and logistics.

introduction.title: What is SAP EWM?
introduction.content: SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) is a warehouse management solution used to control and optimize warehouse operations. It manages inventory movement, storage, picking, packing, shipping and warehouse resources while providing real-time inventory visibility.

business_purpose.title: Why is SAP EWM Used?
business_purpose.content: Warehouses receive raw materials, store inventory and dispatch finished goods. SAP EWM helps improve warehouse efficiency, inventory accuracy and order fulfillment while reducing manual work and operational delays.

warehouse_structure.name: Storage Bin
warehouse_structure.description: The exact physical location where inventory is stored.

business_example.title: Example
business_example.content: Raw materials received from suppliers are recorded through an inbound delivery, inspected by Quality Management, and stored in designated storage bins. When production requires materials, EWM generates warehouse tasks for picking and supplies them to the production line. Finished products are later packed, loaded and dispatched to customers.

best_practices: Use barcode or RF devices where available.
Ensure timely goods receipt and goods issue postings.
